useCases.title: B2B Quote Configuration Agent
statusQuo: Sales engineers manually assemble multi-site MPLS and SD-WAN quotes across spreadsheets and legacy price books.
Each enterprise quote requires back-and-forth email threads with product management to validate discount thresholds.
Misconfigured bandwidth tiers and missing cross-connect charges surface only after the order hits provisioning.
agentification: The agent assembles a validated multi-site quote from the opportunity record, applying current price books and approved discount bands automatically.
It checks serviceability and access technology per site against network inventory before any line item is priced.
It drafts the customer-ready proposal document and routes out-of-policy discounts to the deal desk for approval.

useCases.title: Lead Qualification Scoring Engine

kpis.label: Rep time spent on unqualified leads

statusQuo: Inbound fiber and 5G business leads sit in a shared queue where reps cherry-pick based on gut feel.
Reps manually look up building serviceability and existing account footprint for every lead before dialing.
High-intent leads from coverage-expansion campaigns go cold because scoring rules have not been updated in two years.
agentification: The engine scores each inbound lead on firmographics, serviceability, and usage-intent signals the moment it lands.
It enriches the lead record with building lit status, competitor presence, and existing group-account relationships.
It routes hot leads to the right territory rep and notifies them with a suggested opening talk track.

useCases.title: Site Serviceability Qualification Agent
statusQuo: Solution consultants email the network planning team a spreadsheet of addresses and wait days for lit-building answers.
Address-matching errors between CRM and network inventory produce false negatives that kill viable deals.
Near-net buildings requiring short fiber laterals are quoted as off-net because build-cost data is buried in planning tools.
agentification: The agent qualifies entire multi-site address lists against fiber routes, lit buildings, and fixed-wireless coverage in one pass.
It resolves fuzzy address matches and flags near-net sites with an estimated lateral build cost and interval.
It publishes a color-coded serviceability matrix back to the opportunity and recommends the best access technology per site.

useCases.title: Competitive Win-Loss Analyzer

kpis.label: Competitive win rate in contested deals

statusQuo: Loss reasons are free-text fields that reps fill inconsistently, making pattern analysis nearly impossible.
The quarterly win-loss deck takes three analyst-weeks of manual CRM exports and interview note collation.
Pricing pressure from cable overbuilders is spotted a full quarter after it starts eroding a market.
agentification: The analyzer classifies every closed opportunity by loss driver from CRM notes, call summaries, and quote history.
It detects emerging competitor pricing moves by market and product line within a weekly cadence.
It generates a briefing for sales leadership and recommends counter-plays for the three most contested segments.

useCases.title: Enterprise RFP Response Agent

kpis.label: Bid team overtime hours per RFP

statusQuo: Bid managers rebuild answers to the same SLA, security, and network-architecture questions for every carrier RFP.
The answer library is a shared drive of stale documents where nobody trusts which version reflects current products.
Compliance matrices are assembled by hand the night before submission, and missed mandatory items disqualify bids.
agentification: The agent drafts responses to each RFP question from a curated, version-controlled answer library and current product specs.
It builds the compliance matrix automatically and flags mandatory requirements the standard portfolio cannot meet.
It routes technical and legal exceptions to the right subject-matter owners and tracks sign-off before submission.

useCases.title: Order Fallout Resolution Agent
statusQuo: Failed orders pile up in a fallout queue where specialists triage them one by one across four different swivel-chair systems.
The same address-mismatch and inventory-conflict errors recur daily because nobody has bandwidth to fix root causes.
Customers learn their install date slipped only when they call in, days after the order actually stalled.
agentification: The agent classifies each fallout by error signature and auto-remediates known patterns like address normalization and port conflicts.
It replays corrected orders through the orchestration flow and verifies downstream activation completes.
It escalates unrecognized failure patterns to the fallout team with a full diagnostic trail and suggested fix.

useCases.title: eSIM Activation Orchestrator
statusQuo: Provisioning engineers manually retry stuck eSIM downloads by grepping SM-DP+ logs and re-pushing profiles.
HLR/HSS, entitlement server, and billing states drift out of sync during bulk device-launch weekends.
Failed activations during flagship phone launches create hours-long backlogs that overwhelm the care line.
agentification: The orchestrator watches every activation flow end to end and retries or re-sequences failed profile downloads automatically.
It reconciles subscriber state across HSS, entitlement, and billing before declaring an activation complete.
It notifies the customer with device-specific recovery steps when a handset-side action is the blocker.

useCases.title: Number Porting Exception Agent
statusQuo: Porting analysts manually compare rejected LSR fields against losing-carrier CSRs to find the one mismatched character.
Account-number and ZIP mismatches bounce ports back to the customer, who often abandons the switch entirely.
Wholesale partner porting disputes are tracked in email threads with no visibility into aging or ownership.
agentification: The agent parses each port rejection, identifies the mismatched field, and drafts the corrected resubmission for one-click approval.
It proactively validates port-in requests against carrier CSR data before first submission to prevent rejections.
It escalates ports approaching regulatory interval deadlines with a complete case history attached.

useCases.title: Order Jeopardy Prediction Engine

kpis.label: On-time delivery rate for enterprise circuits

statusQuo: Delivery managers discover a circuit will miss its committed date only when the technician dispatch fails.
Jeopardy status is maintained in a weekly spreadsheet review that covers barely a fifth of the open order book.
Third-party access-provider delays are invisible until the FOC date passes without confirmation.
agentification: The engine scores every in-flight order daily for slip risk using milestone velocity, supplier history, and task aging.
It flags at-risk orders with the specific stalled milestone and the historically fastest recovery path.
It drafts proactive customer delay notifications and creates expedite tasks for the delivery team on high-value circuits.

useCases.title: Legacy Service Migration Orchestrator
statusQuo: Copper-to-fiber and TDM-to-IP migrations are planned in spreadsheets keyed off an inventory system known to be 15% wrong.
Engineers hand-build each migration order, and one mis-mapped VLAN takes a business customer dark for a morning.
Customers get a generic letter and a date, with no coordination against their change-freeze windows.
agentification: The orchestrator batches migration candidates by serving terminal, validates inventory against live network state, and sequences cutovers to minimize risk.
It generates per-customer migration orders with verified service mappings and schedules them around registered change freezes.
It monitors post-cutover service health and automatically rolls back or escalates any circuit failing verification.

useCases.title: Alarm Noise Reduction Engine

kpis.label: NOC alarms handled per engineer shift

statusQuo: NOC engineers wade through tens of thousands of raw alarms per shift, most of them sympathetic noise from a single upstream fault.
Correlation rules are hand-maintained and break every time a new vendor domain or 5G element is added.
Genuine service-affecting alarms sit unacknowledged behind walls of flapping-link noise.
agentification: The engine correlates alarm storms into single root-cause cases using topology, timing, and learned fault signatures.
It suppresses sympathetic and transient alarms while preserving a full evidence trail for audit.
It creates one enriched incident ticket per root cause and routes it to the owning domain team with impact scope attached.

useCases.title: Cell Congestion Forecasting Engine
statusQuo: Capacity planners review utilization reports monthly, so a cell can run hot for weeks before anyone notices.
Sector-level growth forecasting is a spreadsheet extrapolation that misses event-driven and seasonal traffic shifts.
Augment budgets are allocated to whoever complains loudest rather than to the cells with the worst subscriber impact.
agentification: The engine forecasts per-sector PRB and throughput demand weekly, blending trend, seasonality, and local event calendars.
It ranks the augment backlog by predicted subscriber-experience impact and revenue at risk.
It recommends the cheapest sufficient remedy per cell, from parameter rebalancing to carrier adds to new site builds, and publishes the plan to the capacity board.

useCases.title: Fiber Cut Triage Agent

kpis.label: Mean restoration time for backbone cuts

statusQuo: When a backbone fiber is cut, engineers manually cross-reference OTDR traces against splice records to guess the break location.
Determining which customers and mobile sites ride the damaged cable takes an hour of inventory queries.
Restoration crews are dispatched to an approximate roadside location and burn daylight hunting for the actual damage point.
agentification: The agent triangulates the break location from OTDR distance readings mapped onto GIS fiber routes and splice-point records.
It computes the full service-impact blast radius, triggers automatic traffic reroutes where protection paths exist, and opens the master incident.
It dispatches the nearest qualified splicing crew with driving directions to the predicted damage point and notifies affected enterprise customers.

useCases.title: 5G Network Slice SLA Monitor

kpis.label: SLA credits paid to slice customers

statusQuo: Latency and throughput commitments for private 5G slices are checked against next-day reports, long after the breach hurt the customer.
Each enterprise slice customer gets a hand-assembled monthly SLA pack built from raw counter exports.
Nobody can say in real time whether a factory automation slice is meeting its 10ms latency commitment.
agentification: The monitor evaluates per-slice latency, throughput, and availability against contracted SLOs continuously.
It triggers slice reconfiguration or resource reallocation workflows when a KPI trends toward breach.
It generates customer-facing SLA reports automatically and escalates confirmed breaches to the account team with credit exposure calculated.

useCases.title: RAN Parameter Optimization Agent

kpis.label: Drop call rate in optimized clusters

statusQuo: RF engineers tune neighbor lists, tilts, and handover thresholds cluster by cluster using drive-test data that is stale on arrival.
Parameter changes go out in bulk scripts with no automated before/after verification, so regressions linger for weeks.
Conflicting changes from coverage, capacity, and energy-saving teams silently fight each other on the same cells.
agentification: The agent analyzes per-cell performance counters and geolocated call traces to identify the worst-offending parameter sets weekly.
It recommends specific tilt, power, and mobility parameter changes with predicted KPI impact, and stages them for engineer approval.
It verifies post-change KPIs within 24 hours and automatically rolls back any change that degrades accessibility or retention.

useCases.title: Revenue Leakage Detection Analyzer

kpis.label: Revenue leakage as % of billed revenue

statusQuo: Analysts reconcile switch CDR counts against billed events with sampled monthly spreadsheets that miss whole rating streams.
Services provisioned in the network but absent from billing are found only during annual audits, past the back-billing window.
Each new product launch introduces rating gaps that nobody detects until margins mysteriously erode.
agentification: The analyzer reconciles usage records end to end from network mediation through rating to invoice, every night, at full volume.
It detects unbilled active services by cross-matching provisioning inventory against the billing catalog.
It creates prioritized leakage cases with root-cause classification and recoverable-amount estimates for the assurance team.

useCases.title: Bill Dispute Resolution Agent
statusQuo: Billing analysts manually pull invoice lines, contract rates, and usage detail from three systems to adjudicate each dispute.
Under deadline pressure, agents issue goodwill credits without verifying the charge, training customers to dispute everything.
The same proration and contract-rate root causes generate disputes month after month with no feedback loop to billing configuration.
agentification: The agent adjudicates each dispute by comparing the contested charge against contract terms, rate plans, and metered usage.
It drafts a customer-facing resolution letter with line-item evidence for approval or auto-sends within policy thresholds.
It clusters dispute root causes and creates billing-configuration fix tickets to stop recurring dispute classes at the source.

useCases.title: Roaming Settlement Reconciliation Engine
statusQuo: TAP/BCE files from hundreds of roaming partners are reconciled on a 10% sample because full-volume matching is impossible manually.
IOT rate misapplications by partners go unnoticed until they compound across quarters of settlements.
Discrepancy disputes with partners drag across billing cycles because assembling record-level evidence takes weeks.
agentification: The engine reconciles every inbound and outbound roaming record against partner agreements and IOT rate tables each settlement cycle.
It flags rate misapplications, duplicate records, and missing files per partner with quantified financial exposure.
It generates dispute packages with record-level evidence formatted for the partner's settlement process and tracks them to closure.

useCases.title: SIM Swap Fraud Detection Monitor
statusQuo: Fraud analysts review SIM swap alerts from a rules engine hours after the swap, well after account-takeover damage is done.
Static rules flag thousands of legitimate device upgrades, burying the handful of genuine takeover attempts.
Correlating a swap with subsequent high-risk actions like international premium calls requires manual queries across three systems.
agentification: The monitor scores every SIM swap in real time against behavioral signals like recent credential resets, dormancy, and channel anomalies.
It automatically holds high-risk swaps pending step-up verification and applies temporary blocks on premium and international routing.
It escalates confirmed fraud patterns to the fraud team with a complete event timeline and notifies affected customers through a verified channel.

useCases.title: Usage Rating Anomaly Monitor
statusQuo: Rating misconfigurations after catalog changes are caught by angry customers reading their invoices, not by internal controls.
Millions of CDRs sit in rating suspense queues that analysts drain by hand with one-off SQL fixes.
A single bad tariff push once double-rated a full day of data traffic and forced a mass rebill and press cycle.
agentification: The monitor baselines rated revenue per product, plan, and event type daily and flags statistical deviations within hours of a catalog change.
It classifies suspense records by failure cause and auto-reprocesses those matching known correction patterns.
It alerts billing operations before invoice generation when an anomaly would affect a bill run, and recommends holding the impacted cycle.

useCases.title: Churn Save Desk Agent
statusQuo: Save-desk reps offer the same maximum-discount script to every canceling customer regardless of churn reason or value.
Reps have no view of the customer's network experience, so they offer price cuts to customers actually leaving over coverage.
Retention offers are approved via supervisor shoulder-taps, creating hold times that harden the customer's decision.
agentification: The agent surfaces the customer's likely churn driver, lifetime value, and network-experience history to the rep the moment the call connects.
It recommends the minimum-cost save offer ranked by predicted acceptance, from plan right-sizing to device credits to a service fix.
It auto-approves offers within value-based guardrails and books follow-up tasks to verify promised fixes actually landed.

useCases.title: Care Call Resolution Copilot Agent
statusQuo: Agents toggle among seven applications mid-call to see billing, device, network, and order status for one customer.
Troubleshooting quality depends entirely on individual agent tenure, and new hires transfer anything complicated.
After-call work consumes two minutes per contact writing free-text notes nobody can analyze later.
agentification: The copilot assembles a unified customer context panel with billing, outage, device, and order status before the agent says hello.
It suggests the next-best troubleshooting step in real time based on the live conversation and known-issue signatures.
It drafts structured wrap-up notes and disposition codes automatically, and creates follow-up tickets when the issue needs field or engineering work.

useCases.title: Proactive Outage Notification Orchestrator
statusQuo: During outages, thousands of customers call in to tell the operator about an incident the NOC has known about for an hour.
Outage messaging is a manual IVR recording updated by a supervisor when someone remembers.
Customers on the same street get contradictory answers depending on which agent they reach.
agentification: The orchestrator maps each confirmed network incident to the exact affected customer base using service topology.
It notifies affected customers via their preferred channel with cause, ETA, and updates as restoration progresses.
It publishes the incident context to IVR and agent desktops so every touchpoint gives one consistent answer, then confirms restoration with impacted customers.

useCases.title: Complaint Root Cause Analyzer
statusQuo: Contact drivers are inferred from agent disposition codes that are wrong or generic more than half the time.
A billing or firmware defect generates thousands of calls for weeks before anyone connects the dots across regions.
The monthly complaints review is anecdote-driven, so fixes chase the loudest recent story rather than the biggest driver.
agentification: The analyzer mines call transcripts, chat logs, and tickets daily to classify true contact reasons independent of disposition codes.
It detects emerging complaint clusters and correlates them with recent releases, bill runs, and network changes.
It generates a ranked contact-driver report with estimated cost-to-serve and routes defect cases to the owning product or billing team.

useCases.title: NPS Detractor Recovery Agent
statusQuo: Detractor survey responses land in a dashboard that the CX team reviews monthly, long after the frustration peaked.
Free-text feedback is skimmed for anecdotes rather than systematically linked to the account's actual service history.
Closing the loop is left to account managers' spare time, so only the largest accounts ever hear back.
agentification: The agent triages every detractor response within minutes, matching the verbatim against the account's incident, billing, and contact history.
It drafts a personalized recovery outreach with a concrete remedy and routes it to the right owner with a response-time SLA.
It tracks each recovery case to closure and escalates accounts showing churn-risk signals to the retention team.

useCases.title: Dispatch Optimization Orchestrator

kpis.label: Jobs completed per technician per day

statusQuo: Dispatchers hand-shuffle the day's route board every morning and again after every cancellation or overrun.
Skill and equipment mismatches send DSL-certified techs to fiber jobs they cannot complete.
Emergency tickets bump scheduled installs arbitrarily, cascading missed appointments across the rest of the day.
agentification: The orchestrator builds optimized daily routes balancing skills, parts inventory, SLA clocks, and traffic, and re-optimizes continuously as the day unfolds.
It absorbs emergency work by recommending the insertion that minimizes total appointment breakage.
It notifies customers with live technician ETAs and automatically rebooks any job that can no longer be met within its window.

useCases.title: Truck Roll Avoidance Agent
statusQuo: More than a quarter of dispatched technicians find nothing wrong on site because remote diagnostics were never run.
CPE reboots, provisioning refreshes, and line resets that would fix the issue remotely are skipped under call-time pressure.
Every avoidable roll costs a hundred-plus dollars and blocks a slot a genuinely broken customer needed.
agentification: The agent runs a full remote diagnostic battery on the line, CPE, and provisioning state before any dispatch is booked.
It executes safe remote remediations like ONT resets, profile re-pushes, and firmware updates, and verifies service restoration with the customer.
It attaches complete diagnostic results to unavoidable dispatches and recommends the parts the technician should carry.

useCases.title: Fiber Build Permitting Agent
statusQuo: Program managers track thousands of municipal permits across dozens of jurisdictions in a shared spreadsheet with no aging alerts.
Each jurisdiction's application format and traffic-control requirements are tribal knowledge held by one or two coordinators.
Expired permits and missed utility-locate windows idle construction crews at $10K per day.
agentification: The agent drafts permit applications pre-filled with route drawings, traffic plans, and jurisdiction-specific requirements from a maintained rules library.
It tracks every application's status, ages against jurisdiction SLAs, and escalates stalled permits with the required follow-up action.
It sequences build segments around forecast permit-grant dates and alerts crews before locates or permits expire.

useCases.title: Tower Maintenance Scheduling Engine
statusQuo: Preventive maintenance schedules live in an annual spreadsheet that ignores site alarm history and climb-crew availability.
Crews visit the same remote tower three times in a month for separate work orders that could have been bundled.
Backup batteries and generators fail during storm outages because degradation trends were never reviewed.
agentification: The engine builds risk-ranked maintenance schedules from equipment age, alarm trends, environmental telemetry, and storm-season timing.
It bundles compatible work orders per site visit and matches them to certified climb crews and required equipment.
It flags degrading power systems from telemetry trends and creates priority work orders before failure, notifying the planner of sites at highest storm risk.

useCases.title: Field Job Closure Quality Analyzer
statusQuo: Quality auditors manually review a 2% sample of closed jobs, so poor workmanship patterns persist for months.
Closure photos and light-level readings are attached inconsistently and nobody checks them against standards.
Inaccurate as-built updates from rushed closures corrupt the inventory that every downstream order depends on.
agentification: The analyzer audits every closed job by checking photos, signal readings, and test results against workmanship standards.
It scores technicians and crews on closure quality trends and recommends targeted coaching to their supervisors.
It flags jobs with high repeat-dispatch risk for proactive follow-up and creates inventory correction tasks when as-built data conflicts with network records.
